frameworks.name: cortically-focused cognition
frameworks.one_line: view that PFC drives cognition and SC merely executes spatiomotor outputs

frameworks.name: dynamical systems model of SC
frameworks.one_line: SC network dynamics replicate context and choice encoding in the task

core_question: Does the superior colliculus participate in non-spatial cognitive processing such as task-context representation?
key_assumptions: SC is traditionally a spatiomotor structure
PFC is the canonical substrate for cognitive processing
task context and spatial choice are dissociable epochs within a trial
manipulations: rapid switching between task contexts demanding different sensorimotor mappings
bilateral SC silencing across pre-choice and choice epochs
simultaneous recording in SC vs PFC
key_findings: SC encoded task context more strongly than PFC before spatial choice formation
bilateral SC silencing impaired behavior before choices could be formed
SC encoded the upcoming choice faster than PFC once choices began forming
SC silencing no longer impaired performance during the choice epoch
interpretations: SC plays a central role in non-spatiomotor cognitive processing, not merely motor output
cortically-focused views of cognition are challenged by subcortical context encoding
